behaviour.mcq: Select one option. Answer revealed immediately on selection.

questions.stem: Which is NOT a test for the peripheral vestibular system?
questions.explanation: The tuning-fork (fork) test assesses hearing, not the vestibular system. Fistula, rotation and caloric tests are all vestibular.

questions.stem: Which is NOT a complication of middle-ear disease?
questions.explanation: Cavernous sinus thrombosis complicates sinusitis/facial infection. Middle-ear disease causes sigmoid sinus thrombosis, mastoiditis, labyrinthitis and meningitis.

questions.stem: Which is a LOCAL cause of epistaxis?
questions.explanation: Trauma is local. Anticoagulants, bleeding disorders and hypertension are systemic (general) causes.

questions.stem: Which is NOT a cause of a white tongue?
questions.explanation: Fissured tongue = grooves, not a white plaque. Leukoplakia, oral lichen planus and candidiasis all produce white lesions.

questions.stem: Which is NOT an infectious cause of a neck lump?
questions.explanation: Vasomotor rhinitis is non-infectious (and not a neck lump). TB, mumps and HIV are infective causes of cervical swelling.

questions.stem: Which does NOT lead to septal perforation?
questions.explanation: Vasomotor rhinitis doesn’t perforate the septum. Trauma/surgery, cocaine, and Wegener’s (GPA) classically do (also infection, steroid overuse).

questions.stem: Which artery does NOT supply the internal nose?
questions.explanation: The ascending pharyngeal artery supplies the pharynx/tonsil, not the internal nose. The nose is fed by the ethmoidals (from the ophthalmic/ICA) and the sphenopalatine (+ greater palatine, superior labial) from the maxillary/ECA.

questions.stem: Which sinus drains into the superior nasal meatus?
questions.explanation: By elimination: frontal, maxillary and anterior ethmoid all drain to the middle meatus, so the answer here is sphenoid (it drains highest, via the sphenoethmoidal recess). Note: strictly, the posterior ethmoid cells are the true “superior-meatus” sinus — but they’re not offered as an option.

questions.stem: Which hearing test OBJECTIVELY measures the hearing threshold?
questions.explanation: ABR/BERA objectively measures threshold. PTA is subjective; OAE tests outer-hair-cell function; stapedius reflex is objective but not a threshold test.

questions.stem: Squamous cell carcinoma of the mouth most likely arises on the:
questions.explanation: The tongue (lateral/ventral border) and floor of mouth are the highest-risk intra-oral SCC sites.

questions.stem: Which does NOT cause a PAINFUL parotid swelling?
questions.explanation: A neoplasm (esp. benign pleomorphic adenoma) is classically a painless lump — pain is a red flag for malignancy. Mumps and sialolithiasis are painful. Flagged: sarcoidosis (Heerfordt) is also usually painless, so this source item is somewhat ambiguous — "neoplasia" is the expected key.

questions.stem: Allergic rhinitis — choose the WRONG statement:

options.A: Presents with a positive allergy test
options.D: Treated with decongestants, corticosteroids, oral antihistamines

questions.explanation: "Affects 40%" is overstated — allergic rhinitis prevalence is usually quoted ~10–25%. The other three statements are correct. (Mild ambiguity, as some regions report higher figures.)

questions.stem: Which structure is NOT adjacent to the sphenoid sinus?
questions.explanation: The mandibular nerve (V3) exits via foramen ovale into the infratemporal fossa — not related to the sphenoid sinus. The ICA, CN III (cavernous sinus wall) and V2 are all adjacent (plus optic nerve, cavernous sinus, Vidian nerve).

questions.stem: Which is NOT true of trigeminal neuralgia?

options.A: Ipsilateral lacrimation and rhinorrhoea are common
options.C: The ophthalmic division is most often affected

questions.explanation: The ophthalmic (V1) division is the LEAST affected — the maxillary (V2) and mandibular (V3) divisions are most common, so that statement is false. Carbamazepine is the first-line drug. Flagged: the lacrimation/rhinorrhoea option is also atypical of classic TN, so more than one answer is arguable.

questions.stem: Which EARLY symptom presents with a hypopharyngeal tumour?
questions.explanation: Sore throat / throat discomfort is the typical early symptom (with dysphagia). Referred otalgia is also early/characteristic; dysphonia (laryngeal spread) and weight loss are late. Mild ambiguity between sore throat and referred otalgia.

questions.stem: Which is NOT a cause of chronic laryngitis?
questions.explanation: Hypothyroidism causes hoarseness via myxoedema/Reinke-type changes but is not classed as chronic laryngitis. LPR, smoking and vocal abuse are the classic causes.

questions.stem: Which is NOT a symptom of Ménière’s disease?
questions.explanation: Ménière’s tetrad = vertigo + fluctuating SNHL + tinnitus + aural fullness. Headache is not part of it.
